summary: Delivered high-performance platform capabilities that scaled payment and data services, reducing latency and accelerating release cadence across production systems. Seven years building Python and Go microservices, deploying on AWS with Kubernetes and Terraform, and improving CI/CD pipelines and observability. Experienced in PostgreSQL, Redis and distributed messaging patterns, with pragmatic automation and monitoring focus. Comfortable working in Agile/Scrum environments to produce reliable, scalable backend services for platform teams.

responsibilities: Build scalable Python microservices on Kubernetes, reducing API latency by 40% across payments processing.
Lead an 8-engineer effort to migrate monolith to services, improving deployment frequency by 3x.
Design REST APIs and PostgreSQL schemas ensuring consistent request validation and fault-tolerant data flows.
Improve CI/CD pipelines using Terraform and Kubernetes, enhancing rollback safety and build parallelism.
Deploy containerized services with Docker and Helm, managing autoscaling and reducing costs by 25%.
Built ETL pipelines in Python ingesting data from 12 enterprise sources into PostgreSQL warehouses.
Designed streaming integrations using Kafka and Redis to reduce processing latency by 55% across reports.
Led migration of legacy pipelines to Docker containers, enabling consistent deployments across multiple environments.
Reduced data processing costs by 30% through query optimization and partitioning of large transactional tables.
Automated CI/CD for data workflows with Terraform and Kubernetes, improving deployment reliability and auditability.
